gaps: No existing test case validates the product name displayed on onboarding screens — all onboarding-related tests (66363536) only verify that tapping a device shows 'correct onboarding information' but never explicitly check that the displayed name matches the correct Poly product name vs. an internal code name.

No test case validates onboarding screen content after a factory reset — the bug triggers on first launch OR after factory reset, but no existing test covers the factory-reset-triggered onboarding flow.

No test case validates the onboarding screens for the Voyager Free 60+ UC (or any specific Poly device model) to ensure the correct commercial product name is displayed instead of internal code names like 'Falcon'.
No test case checks for internal code name leakage in any UI surface — there is no negative testing pattern that validates that internal/codename strings are not shown to end users.

No test case covers the onboarding flow at all for content accuracy — test 66363536 only checks that tapping a device 'displays correct onboarding information' but does not define what 'correct' means in terms of product naming, and has no assertion against the commercial product name.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] Voyager Free 60+ UC — Verify commercial product name displayed (not internal codename 'Falcon')
steps: Factory reset the Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds (hold touchscreen case button 10s or use case menu: Settings → Factory Reset).
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 60+ UC device when it appears in the device list.
Observe the onboarding screen(s) displayed.
Screenshot the onboarding screen(s).
generated_testcases.expected: The onboarding screen(s) display the commercial product name 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC' prominently. The internal code name 'Falcon' does NOT appear anywhere on any onboarding screen. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, the onboarding header or body text shows 'Falcon' instead of 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC'.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] First launch after factory reset — Verify product name on all onboarding pages
steps: Factory reset the Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds.
Clear Poly Studio Mobile app data (Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Storage → Clear data).
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Complete Bluetooth pairing flow if prompted.
Tap the Voyager Free 60+ UC device.
Swipe through ALL onboarding pages/screens until the final 'Done' or 'Get Started' button.
For each onboarding page, capture the product name text displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: Every onboarding page displays the commercial product name 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC'. No page displays 'Falcon' or any other internal code name. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, the first onboarding page (or the header) shows 'Falcon' as the device name, especially on the factory-reset-triggered onboarding flow.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] App reinstall — Verify no internal codename leakage on onboarding
steps: Uninstall Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reboot phone.
Install Poly Studio Mobile app fresh from poly.com-software or Play Store.
Launch app and grant all permissions.
Pair Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds (factory reset state).
Tap the device to trigger onboarding.
Inspect all text elements on all onboarding screens.
generated_testcases.expected: All onboarding screens use the commercial product name 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC'. No occurrence of 'Falcon', 'VF60', or any internal engineering codename is visible. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, the onboarding title or subtitle contains 'Falcon' as a placeholder string that was not replaced with the commercial name.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] Negative test — Assert no internal codename strings in any onboarding UI element
steps: Factory reset Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and pair the device.
Tap the device to enter onboarding flow.
Using UI Automator Viewer or Appium, extract all visible text elements (TextView, Button, ImageView content descriptions) from all onboarding screens.
Search extracted strings for: 'Falcon', 'falcon', 'FALCON', 'VF60', 'vf60', 'voyager_free_60', 'free60'.
Repeat for any tooltip, subtitle, or accessibility label.
generated_testcases.expected: None of the internal codename strings ('Falcon', 'VF60', 'voyager_free_60', 'free60') appear in any visible UI element, accessibility label, or content description on any onboarding screen. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, at least one UI element contains 'Falcon' (case-insensitive) as its displayed text or content description.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] Voyager Free 60+ UC — Verify product name persists across onboarding → dismiss → re-onboarding
steps: Factory reset Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and pair the device.
Tap device → observe onboarding screen 1 → verify product name is 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC'.
Tap 'Skip' or back out of onboarding (do NOT complete it).
Kill the app (swipe from recents).
Reopen app → tap the device again → observe onboarding screen 1.
Verify product name again.
generated_testcases.expected: Onboarding screen 1 displays 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC' both before and after app kill. No internal codename appears. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, the product name shows 'Falcon' on the first onboarding screen after re-launching the app.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] Device re-pair after factory reset — Verify onboarding content accuracy for Voyager Free 60+ UC
steps: Factory reset Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → pair device → complete onboarding fully (tap 'Done').
Navigate to Device → Audio Settings → verify settings page loads correctly.
Go back to device list → forget the device (Settings → Forget Device).
Factory reset earbuds again.
Re-pair the earbuds with the app.
Tap device → observe onboarding screens again.
Verify product name on all screens.
generated_testcases.expected: Onboarding screens display 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC' on every re-pair cycle. No internal codename 'Falcon' appears. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, the second onboarding flow after re-pair shows 'Falcon' instead of the commercial name, indicating the codename is cached or not properly replaced on repeated factory-reset flows.

generated_testcases.title: [Onboarding] IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER — Verify product name on onboarding when app is killed mid-flow
steps: Factory reset Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app → pair device → tap device to start onboarding.
On onboarding screen 1, IMMEDIATELY kill the app (force-stop via Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Force Stop) WITHOUT navigating to any other screen.
Reopen the app.
Tap the device again.
Observe the onboarding screen(s).
generated_testcases.expected: The onboarding screen(s) display 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC'. No internal codename 'Falcon' appears. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE (Anti-Regression): If the bug is present, killing the app mid-onboarding causes the product name to fall back to the internal codename 'Falcon' on re-launch, because the commercial name was not yet persisted to the onboarding state.
